user_input: from the expandly-poster-faq-link, add orange tone to it. this one is much more cleaner than the recent one. also the clarification at the bottom should be 1 liner

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command '$src = Get-ChildItem -Path "$env:USERPROFILE'"\\.codex\\generated_images\\proj-002\" -Filter *.png | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1
if (-not "'$src) { throw '"'No generated PNG found in expected Codex image output folder.' }
"'$dest = Join-Path (Get-Location) '"'expandly-poster-faq-link-register-orange.png'
Copy-Item -LiteralPath "'$src.FullName -Destination $dest -Force
Get-Item -LiteralPath $dest | Select-Object Name, Length, LastWriteTime, FullName'

content:
I’ll base this on `expandly-poster-faq-link-register-muted-apps.png`, not the busier recent version. The changes are constrained: add stronger orange tone, keep the cleaner layout, and make the bottom clarification a single line.The clean source has the right structure: title, five Q&A rows, muted app grid, big CTA, and a two-line disclaimer. I’m keeping that composition and shifting orange into the number rail, price row, CTA split, and logo corners so it feels branded without becoming noisy.
The revision is generated. I’m placing the output into this project folder as a new file so the cleaner original remains available for comparison.The new PNG is now in the project as `expandly-poster-faq-link-register-orange.png`. I’m checking it visually before I mark it done, specifically for the one-line clarification and whether the orange is prominent without losing the cleaner layout.
